issue_body:
Build the user-facing flow that lets a creator save their customized podcast layout and style as a named show template, then pick that saved template when starting a future episode. The template should carry the show identity forward while still adapting to the current episode's assigned speakers, so repeat creators and agencies can reuse formats instead of rebuilding them each time.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A reviewer can create or customize an episode layout/style, save it as a named show template, see it appear in a template library, select that saved template in a new episode setup or style step, and observe the episode preview/canvas update with the saved layout and visual settings while keeping the current episode's speaker assignments. The existing verification command `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes and includes coverage for saving, listing, selecting, and applying a reusable show template.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The product already has setup, preset styles, a canvas editor, audio polish controls, and visual moments. The next missing foundation in the vision is repeatable show identity: creators need to preserve a finished look across episodes before export becomes genuinely valuable._
